items.title: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent success of agent swarms has shifted the paradigm of large language model (LLM)-based agents from single-agent workflows to multi-agent systems, highlighting the importance of agent orchestration for task decomposition …

items.title: WorkBench Revisited: Workplace Agents Two Years On
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The best agent on WorkBench in March 2024, GPT-4, completed 43% of tasks and took an unintended harmful action, such as emailing the wrong person, on 26% of them. We re-visit the benchmark in June 2026 and find that the best agen…

items.title: YeasierAgent: Agentic Social Sandbox as a Canvas for Intent-Driven Creation of Platform-Agnostic Symbiotic Agent-Native Applications
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces YeasierAgent, an application-building paradigm based on symbiotic agents, narrative worlds, and scene-aware interaction. It challenges the conventional device-coupled model of software by redefining applicat…

items.title: TwinBI: An Agentic Digital Twin for Efficient Augmented Interactions with Business Intelligence Dashboards
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13731v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Business intelligence (BI) increasingly combines dashboard interaction with LLM-based assistance, but these two modes often fall out of sync during multi-step analysis. As users switch between direct dashboard manipulation and na…

items.title: When Sample Selection Bias Precipitates Model Collapse
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13732v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The proliferation of recursive training on synthetic data can alleviate data scarcity but risks model collapse, where repeated training erodes distributional tails and homogenizes outputs. Data selection is widely viewed as a rem…

items.title: Capability Minimization as a Safety Primitive: Risk-Aware Causal Gating for Least-Privilege LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13884v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern decision systems increasingly rely on learned components whose outputs may be confident yet wrong, exposing downstream actions to costly errors. We introduce Risk-Aware Causal Gating (RACG), a framework that decides whethe…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometry problem generation is useful for AI-assisted education and multimodal mathematical reasoning, but reliable synthesis remains difficult because the problem statement, diagram, constraints, and solution should be mutually …

items.title: Dense Coordinate-List Fine-Tuning Induces a Controllable Interference Surface in Vision-Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fine-tuning vision-language models to emit dense coordinate lists improves visual grounding but also changes how models serialize, repeat, and terminate structured outputs. We study this behavior as a generation and control surfa…

items.title: CineOrchestra: Unified Entity-Centric Conditioning for Cinematic Video Generation
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13768v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Cinematic video depicts multiple subjects acting or interacting at specific moments, captured with deliberate camera movement, and stitched together by shot transitions. Together, these elements demand a level of fine-grained c…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.13858v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recommendation systems are essential in modern music streaming platforms due to the vast amount of available content. While collaborative filtering is widely used to suggest items based on the preferences of others with similar…

items.title: Mirage Probes: How Vision Models Fake Visual Understanding
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13870v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) can answer image-based questions confidently, and often correctly, even when no image is provided. This mirage behavior inflates benchmark scores without reflecting visual grounding. Prior work tre…

items.title: How do Self-Supervised Remote Sensing Vision Models Transfer to Downstream Tasks?
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13896v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Self-supervised geospatial foundation models (GeoFMs) learn transferable representations from remote sensing data, but their downstream behavior is difficult to characterize. We study six representative GeoFMs spanning joint-em…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.13710v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep research and agent evolution serve as de-facto tasks for AI agents in real-world applications toward artificial general intelligence. The former enables autonomous retrieval and integration of information in open-ended envir…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.13734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent evidence reported by Tully, Longoni, and Appel (2025) suggests that lower artificial intelligence (AI) literacy predicts greater receptivity toward AI. We revisit this claim using the public data from Study 3 of that artic…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.13916v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Each year, college admissions offices face an overwhelming challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts, each with unique formats, grading systems, and layouts. This manual process creates operational bottlenecks tha…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.14000v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has demonstrated that coding agents can formalize entire advanced mathematics textbooks in Lean 4, yet existing efforts concentrate on branches of mathematics already well-represented in mathlib and measure success so…

items.summary: arXiv:2606.14211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed as agents that interact with external environments and observe feedback such as execution results, error messages, and tool outputs. A well-functioning agent should be able to leverage this feedback…

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.
